items.headline: City Attorney position open; litigation over water quality ongoing
items.summary: Mountain View is recruiting a new City Attorney. The city is also defending a lawsuit brought by San Francisco Baykeeper challenging water quality or pollution practices; the case is pending in federal court in San Jose (Case No. 20-CV-00824-EJD).

items.headline: Planning Commission to study Housing Element update
items.summary: The Planning Commission scheduled a study session on updates to Cupertino's Housing Element. The agenda note cuts off before describing what changes are being considered, so specifics are not available from this document.

items.headline: New restaurant 'Ribbit' permitted for University Avenue
items.summary: A new restaurant or food concept called Ribbit received a permit to install its exterior blade sign in downtown Palo Alto's commercial corridor. The neon green aluminum sign will mark the business's arrival at 364 University Ave.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Planning Commission will hold a study session on an artificial turf policy. No details on the policy's scope, proposed restrictions, or affected areas were provided in the agenda.

items.headline: Six ADU and JADU permits issued in single day
items.summary: San José issued permits for four ADUs, one JADU, and one garage conversion across different neighborhoods on the same date.

items.headline: Foxconn receives $2.4M permit for tool installation in San José
items.summary: The electronics manufacturer cleared to install tooling equipment at its Automation Parkway facility. The project's size and Foxconn's presence signal continued investment in San José's industrial base.

items.headline: City acquires property at 26-36 South First Street
items.summary: San José City Council took action on the acquisition of real property at 26-36 South First Street. The agenda does not specify the purchase price, intended use, or development plans.

items.headline: Housing Department loan portfolio under audit review
items.summary: City Council reviewed an audit report of the Housing Department's loan portfolio. The agenda does not detail findings, loan amounts, or any recommended changes.
